steps.title: Use a two-point exponential setup
steps.content: A $4\%$ annual increase means one year after the starting point the population is $5000(1.04)=5200$. Use the values at $x=0$ and $x=1$ directly.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: In an exponential model $p=a(b)^x$, the value at $x=0$ is $a$, so $a=5000$. A $4\%$ annual increase means the growth factor is $b=1+0.04=1.04$. The model is $p=5000(1.04)^x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $0.96$ is a decay factor and $(5000)^x$ is not the right growth form. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: uses $5000$ as the base raised to $x$, so the initial value is wrong. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: uses $0.96$, a $4\%$ decrease. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: initial population $5000$ multiplied by growth factor $1.04$ each year. Correct.
Choice D is correct.
